attributes.description: Formal salutation for written correspondence (e.g., "Mr. and Mrs. Smith").
attributes.name: formalGreeting
attributes.usage: Drives mail-merge in donor acknowledgment letters and major appeals. NPSP auto-generates from member contact names; manually editable.

attributes.description: Informal salutation for casual correspondence (e.g., "John and Jane").
attributes.name: informalGreeting
attributes.usage: Drives less-formal communications. Distinct from formal for tone control.

attributes.description: Number of contact records that are members of this household.
attributes.name: householdMembers
attributes.usage: Auto-derived from contacts where AccountId points to this Household. Used for household-size segmentation.

attributes.description: Sum of all donations attributed to household members.
attributes.name: totalLifetimeGiving
attributes.usage: The marquee household-level rollup. Auto-calculated by NPSP as sum of donations linked to any Contact whose AccountId matches this Household. Drives major-donor recognition tiers.

business_context: This subclass captures the NPSP modeling pattern where Household is implemented as one of three Account Record Types (Household, 1-to-1, Organization). NPSP is by far the dominant deployment of this pattern in production. Salesforce vanilla and the B2B systems would implement Household via custom Account record types if at all. The companion Tier 4 household.yaml entity captures the alternative pattern (Household as its own first-class entity, as in CiviCRM and Bloomerang). V2 uses the catalog mechanism field to know which deployment pattern to apply per target backend at deploy time. The two catalog entries cross- reference but don't duplicate — household.yaml has the universal attribute set; this subclass captures the Account-specific delta and the deploy-time mechanism.
name: Household (Account subclass)
purpose: An Account subclass representing a family or living unit, used in CRMs that model households as a Record Type or subtype on Account rather than as a separate first-class entity. Cross-references the household.yaml Tier 4 entity, which captures the same concept where it is modeled as a distinct entity (Bloomerang via Relationships, CiviCRM via Contact subtype).

relationships.description: A household account contains many member contacts.

systems.name: Custom Household object or Type attribute on Companies
systems.notes: Attio supports either a custom Household object or a Type attribute on the Companies object.

systems.name: (handled at Tier 4 entity via Relationships, not as Account subclass)
systems.notes: Bloomerang has no Account entity. Households are modeled via Relationships between individual Constituents. See household.yaml for Bloomerang modeling.

systems.name: (handled at Tier 4 entity, not as Account subclass)
systems.notes: CiviCRM handles Household as a Contact subtype, not as an Account subclass. See household.yaml for CiviCRM modeling.

systems.name: Account with type=Household (custom enum value)
systems.notes: Add a Household enum value to Account.type. No native modeling.

systems.name: Company with custom Type=Household
systems.notes: HubSpot has no Record Type concept. Households are represented via custom Type property on Company.

systems.name: Account with Household Record Type (custom)
systems.notes: Customers without NPSP add a custom Household Record Type to Account. Less common than full NPSP installation.

systems.name: Account with Household Record Type
systems.notes: NPSP's dominant pattern. Household Account is the default Record Type when a Contact is created. Auto-generated household name, formal/informal greeting, and shared mailing address are all NPSP standard fields on Household-typed accounts.
